title: What I Learned From My Biggest Losers Of 2022
text: 2022 was a tough year for all of us. REITs dropped particularly heavily.

title: My 5 Favorite, Undervalued Investments For Year-End 2022
text: The time has come to take a look at my portfolio and see where to cut, and where to invest. The market is ripe for investing, and there are opportunities. There are opportunities in almost every sector out there. In this article, I'll show you some of my current favorite ones.

title: 10 REITs for Passive Income in 2023
text: These 10 REITs are all high-quality and trading at great valuations.

title: My Top REIT Picks For 2023
text: This is the first time in over a decade that my top picks lost money. I'm always happy to show you the proof of my stock-market wins. But I have to be just as honest about the losses.

title: AvalonBay Near 52-Week Low Is An Excellent Opportunity
text: AvalonBay continues its long-term track record of robust operating fundamentals. It has a sizeable development pipeline and is taking advantage of a decent investment spread on asset dispositions.

title: AvalonBay: A Blue-Chip REIT On My Christmas List
text: AvalonBay is the largest apartment REIT on the market today backed by a strong A rated balance sheet. The company continues to have strong pricing power in which they saw nearly 12% rental revenue growth in Q3, almost entirely driven by rental increases.

title: AvalonBay Communities, Inc. Completes $350 Million Senior Notes Offering
text: ARLINGTON, Va.--( BUSINESS WIRE )--AVALONBAY COMMUNITIES, INC. (NYSE: AVB) (the “Company”) announced today that it completed an underwritten public offering of $350 million aggregate principal amount of senior unsecured notes due 2033 (the “Notes”). title: Here's Why You Should Retain AvalonBay (AVB) Stock for Now
text: AvalonBay (AVB) is well-poised to benefit from improving demand, portfolio expansion efforts and technological innovations. A flexible working environment and interest rate hikes are concerning.

title: It's Over: The REIT Market Is Collapsing (Debunked)
text: REITs are dropping more day after day due to rising interest rates. Investors fear that the profitability and asset values will collapse.
